React.definition: I originally learned React in depth with Coursera's Front-End Software Engineer certification by Meta and I have used it in multiple personal projects, this website included, and a commercial project that is also the capstone project for my B.S. of Computer Science.

.NET.definition: .NET is one of the main technologies I have used in my early professional career. With the years of experience I have in .NET, I have used it in a variety of projects including web applications, desktop applications, mobile applications and web services.

.NET Web API.definition: This is the primary framework I have developed with during my time at Dell and Lone Star UAS. I have created many new endpoints and implennted further logic into existing endpoints.

MAUI.definition: I used .NET MAUI in my professional internship when it was fresh of the press as the latest and greatest multi-platform framework, I utilized it to build a multi-platform application for my team.

ASP.NET Core MVC.definition: This is the first framework I learned within .NET and it has been a great tool to build web applications. I have contributed a good amount to our primary web application at my internship, LoneStarWeb, Lone Star UAS's internal web application built on ASP.NET Core MVC.

C++.definition: C++ was my first ever programming language and it will always have a special place in my heart because of that. I have multiple years of classroom and theoretical experience but no work experience with this language yet.

C#.definition: C# is was primary language I have used for my early professional career, I have used in in countless projects from Dell, Lone Star UAS, and my own personal projects. I have used C# in web applications, desktop applications, mobile applications, web services, and APIs.

Python.definition: I have used Python in a couple professional AI projects at Dell

JavaScript.definition: JavaScript is definitely one of my top used languages as it is used in almost every web application I have worked on. I have used JavaScript in both front-end and back-end development and coming from a C++ background, this language was easy to pick up.

CSS.definition: CSS is a skill that I have not necessarily needed to learn in depth, as I have used frameworks such as Bootstrap and Mantine to help me with styling.

HTML.definition: This is one is an obvious skill, every web developer must know HTML and typically learns it early on.

JSX.definition: I have obtained all my JSX experience from React and I have a good understanding of how to use it.

Razor.definition: I have used Razor pages in ASP.NET Core MVC and I have enjoyed the resourcefulness of the C# injection into HTML.

SQL.definition: I have used SQL in a few different projects including a personal project and a few projects at Lone Star UAS. These projects consisted of all the basic CRUD operations and I have a good understanding of how to use SQL.

Git.definition: I use Git version control on all my projects and even important folders that I want to keep track of. I have used Git in both the command line and in Visual Studio.

GitHub.definition: I have used GitHub professionally and for all my personal projects and some important folders that I want to keep track of.

GitLabs.definition: I've used GitLab version control for all my professional projects at Lone Star UAS.

Azure.definition: Azure is a cloud service that I have used to host a few professional projects with my LLC, Carver Tech LLC, along with the organizations database and identity provider.

Docker.definition: Docker is my primary go-to containerization tool

Linux.definition: I've used Linux when needed but I do not have a lot of experience with it.

Visual Studio.definition: Visual Studio was my go-to IDE for most my early career projects. I have used it for almost all my professional projects at Dell, Lone Star UAS, and my personal projects.

Visual Studio Code.definition: I use VS Code for a lot of my microservices and small projects that do not need the full power of Visual Studio.

Xamarin.definition: The experience I have with Xamarin comes from the Xamarin integration in .NET MAUI and the projects I have worked on in .NET MAUI. I have not used Xamarin standalone but I don't forsee any problems if I needed to.

JSON.definition: Who's Jason and why's he always objecting?

YAML.definition: In every CI/CD pipeline I have created, I have used YAML for the configuration.

Jest.definition: I've tested a few React components with Jest and have a good understanding of how to use it.

xUnit.definition: I've used xUnit to create hundreds of unit tests for various endpoints at Dell

Bootstrap.definition: Bootstrap was my first CSS framework I have worked with and I enjoy the ease of use it provides.

Mantine.definition: This website was built with Mantine and it is by far one of my favorite component frameworks I've seen.

MUI.definition: I've used MUI for a few projects and I like the design of the library.

MATLAB.definition: MATLAB has been mostly used for my college classes but I have used it for a few projects including an image processing project using custom kernels.

Version Control.definition: Version Control is one of those skills that is a must for any developer and I have used it in all my projects. Once you learn version control, you learn you cannot live without it.

Java.definition: Java is the primary language I have used for my professional career have support various releases of Java frameworks and applications.

JQuery.definition: JQuery has definitely been one of my favorite JavaScript libraries to use because of it's capabilities.

AJAX.definition: I enjoy using AJAX for the ease of making API calls and web requests.

Entity Framework Core.definition: Entity Framework has definitely been a tool I have used quite often for database connections and migrations.

Stored Procedures.definition: The majority of my experience with SQL comes from creating and using stored procedures. Stored procedures are a goto for complex queries.

Package Managers.definition: The use of package managers have been a great tool for me to use in my projects. I have used NPM, NuGet, and Yarn and I have a good understanding of how to use them.

NPM.definition: NPM is just one of the many package managers I have used.

Yarn.definition: Yarn is just one of the many package managers I have used.

NuGet.definition: NuGet is just one of the many package managers I have used.

Datatables.definition: Datatables is a goto for when I need to display data in a table format. this library is very easy to use and I have used it in multiple projects.

CLI.definition: I'd say I know may way around the CLI pretty well.

TypeScript.definition: I have used TypeScript in a few projects and I do enjoy the type safety it provides and its smooth transition from JavaScript.

Postman.definition: I have used postman countless times to test API calls and it has been a resourceful tool.

APIS.definition: I know the ins and outs of APIs whether it's building them or consuming them. I am familiar with RESTful APIs.

MQTT.definition: MQTT was a fun protocol to learn and I have used it in a few projects. This protocol is great as a bridge between components in a system and is easy to pick up.

ActiveMQ.definition: ActiveMQ is the broker I would use as the MQTT message broker for my projects.

NextJS.definition: I use NextJS for most of my personal projects.

CI/CD.definition: I've implemented multiple CI/CD pipelines and have added and fixed countless jobs and pipelienes.

Harness.definition: Harness has became the main pipeline system at PayPal and I know my way around configuring and running these pipeline both in the UI and defining them as code.

Cursor.definition: I would consider myself a Cursor poweruser, I have tested and utilized every single feature of Cursor be it in the IDE, or as a Cloud agent in the browser both professionally and personally. I also enjoy reading Cursor's product and research blogs.

TerraForm.definition: TerraForm is my go to choice when provisioning infrastructure. I believe especially in the age of Agentic operations, your infrastructure should be declaritive and recorded as code with state.

HashiCorp Vault.definition: I have personally managed several enterprise-wide Vault clusters at PayPal, onboarding multiple teams to their own secret engine paths via TerraForm, have held a relationship with the vendor, HashiCorp, attended HashiConf and am a HashiCorp Vault certified associate.

Spring boot.definition: I have supported various spring boot applications in my professional career.

Internal Frameworks.definition: I have supported the release of multiple child libraries as a part of an enterprise-wide parent java framework along with new forward facing cloud native cryptography libraries.

Cryptography.definition: I have maintained PayPal's internal Key Management System, its supporting libraries, HashiCorp Vault clusters, and various encryption-as-as-service systems and libraries.

Key Management System.definition: I have maintained PayPal's internal Key Management System, its supporting libraries, HashiCorp Vault clusters.

GCP.definition: I have been a major driver on PayPal's journey to become a cloud native company and I have released multiple GCP native cryptographic libraries and designs for the enterprise.

Leadership.definition: I have operated as a team-lead on multiple major enterprise-wide initiatives, driving architecture and designs, and leading teams of contractors to implement our designs. I have also received countless peer-feedback on my impressive leadership, collaboration, and communication abilities.

Incident Response / L1 On-call.definition: I have operated as L1, critical on-call support for PayPal's Key Management and encryption-as-a-serivce legacy infrastructure and have responded and resolved countless incidents.

Developer Support / L2.definition: My team at PayPal owns the Key Management System for the enterprise and we have a developer support channel for developer issues with thousands of developers and I have operated as a primary support on a rotational basis.

Migrations.definition: I have drove major enterprise-wide migrations, coordinating across hundreds of teams, emailing and creating slack channels, and supporting the migration of tens of thousands of Fortanix HSMs to GCP CMEK.

Hiring.definition: I have conducted numerous technical and behavioral interviews for contract and full time positions within our organization.

Onboarding.definition: I have onboarded multiple new members and managers to my team at PayPal by providing documentation, demos, resources, and support.

IntelliJ IDEA.definition: This has been my primary IDE at PayPal for developing in several repositories.

Agentic Development.definition: This is the new era of software development and I have been staying at the forefront of it keeping up to date with the latest tooling and pro tips on how to leverage agents in my developer workflows.
